title: Reporting income & household changes after you're enrolled
meta-title: Reporting income, household, and other changes
meta-description: Changes might affect health insurance coverage, must report ASAP. Income changes, major life changes - understand your choices & responsibilities.
page_name: Reporting income & household changes after you're enrolled
excerpt: Changes might affect health insurance coverage, must report ASAP. Income changes, major life changes - understand your choices & responsibilities.
content:
If you're enrolled in a Marketplace plan and your income or household changes, update your application as soon as possible.

These changes — like higher or lower income, adding or losing household members, or getting offers of other health coverage — may affect the coverage or savings you’re eligible for. After you finish applying or enrolling, you may be asked to submit documents to confirm your income.

Discover which changes to report.

Learn how to report changes.

Why it’s important to update your application right away

If your income estimate goes up or you lose a household member — You may qualify for less savings than you’re getting now. If you don’t report the change and use the advance premium tax credit to lower your monthly premiums, you'll have to pay the difference back between the amount you used and the amount you actually qualify for when you file your federal tax return.

If your income estimate goes down or you gain a household member — You could qualify for:

  • More savings than you’re getting now. This could lower your monthly premiums.
  • Free or low-cost coverage through Medicaid or the Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP). Learn more about Medicaid and CHIP.

Get more information on the premium tax credit from the IRS.

If you need to cancel your plan

Some changes — like starting Medicare coverage or getting a job-based insurance offer — require you to cancel your Marketplace plan. Or you may want to cancel coverage for another reason.

Learn how to cancel your plan.
